title: Antennaria semidecurrens
taxonomy: Antennaria semidecurrens is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Antennaria, which consists of several species of plants commonly known as polar roses or flowering plants. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family includes many well-known species such as sunflowers, daisies, and lettuces. Antennaria semidecurrens is native to North America.

intro: Antennaria semidecurrens, also known as mountain everlasting or rosy everlasting, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and immune system support. The bioactive constituents of Antennaria semidecurrens include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
